content.verdict: Porter and Zeabur both automate Git-based deployment, but Porter runs applications inside the customer's own AWS, GCP, or Azure account with usage-based per-resource pricing and SOC 2/HIPAA compliance, while Zeabur deploys via its own multi-cloud-backed compute with a free starter tier, low-cost tiered plans, and an AI Agent for natural-language infrastructure management.
content.bestForToolA: Teams that need apps running inside their own cloud account — for cost control, compliance, or existing cloud commitments — and want SOC 2 Type II or one-click HIPAA compliance with per-PR preview environments and zero-downtime Kubernetes upgrades.
content.bestForToolB: Smaller teams or solo developers wanting a free tier to start, low-cost tiered plans ($5–$79/month), automatic language/framework detection, and natural-language infrastructure management via the Zeabur Agent.
content.whoNeedsBoth: A startup might prototype quickly on Zeabur's free or Dev tier, then migrate production, compliance-sensitive workloads to Porter once it needs to run inside its own AWS, GCP, or Azure account with SOC 2/HIPAA coverage.

keyDifferences.title: Where applications run
keyDifferences.toolA: Deploys applications inside the customer's own AWS, GCP, or Azure account.
keyDifferences.toolB: Deploys dedicated compute across providers including AWS, Hetzner, and Linode, without stating apps run in the customer's own account.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Organizations with existing cloud commitments or data-residency requirements tied to their own account need that specific deployment model.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Enterprises consolidating billing and compliance under their existing cloud provider contract.

keyDifferences.title: Compliance certification
keyDifferences.toolA: SOC 2 Type II certified, with a one-click HIPAA compliance option.
keyDifferences.toolB: No SOC 2 or HIPAA certification is documented.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Regulated industries need documented platform-level certifications.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Healthcare and fintech teams with compliance requirements.

keyDifferences.title: Pricing model
keyDifferences.toolA: Usage-based per-resource fees ($6/month per GB RAM + $13/month per vCPU) on top of underlying cloud costs, with no general free tier (only a startup program offering 6 months free for eligible companies).
keyDifferences.toolB: Offers a genuine $0 Free plan plus flat tiered plans at $5, $19, and $79 per month.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Cost-sensitive early-stage teams need a way to start at zero cost.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Bootstrapped startups and solo developers wanting to start free.

keyDifferences.title: AI-driven infrastructure management
keyDifferences.toolA: No natural-language AI agent for infrastructure management is documented.
keyDifferences.toolB: Includes the Zeabur Agent, which turns natural-language requests into infrastructure actions like renting servers or deploying services, with full access on the Pro plan ($19/month) or higher.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Non-expert users benefit from conversational infrastructure control instead of manual configuration.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Smaller teams without dedicated DevOps staff.

keyDifferences.title: Preview environments
keyDifferences.toolA: Automatically generates isolated preview environments for every pull request.
keyDifferences.toolB: No per-PR preview environment feature is documented.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams with active PR-based review workflows benefit from automatic, isolated preview deployments.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Engineering teams doing frequent code review before merge.

faqs.question: Where does Porter run applications?
faqs.answer: Inside the customer's own AWS, GCP, or Azure cloud account, not a shared Porter-hosted environment.

faqs.question: How is Porter priced?
faqs.answer: Usage-based: $6/month per GB of RAM and $13/month per vCPU, on the Standard plan, plus underlying cloud provider costs.

faqs.question: Does Porter support HIPAA compliance?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers a one-click HIPAA compliance option alongside SOC 2 Type II certification.

faqs.question: Does Zeabur have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zeabur offers a Free plan with one manageable server, community support, and 48-hour log retention.

faqs.question: What is the Zeabur AI Agent?
faqs.answer: It turns natural-language instructions into infrastructure actions such as renting servers, deploying services, or accessing AI models; full access requires the Pro plan.

faqs.question: Can I deploy across multiple cloud providers with Zeabur?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zeabur supports dedicated compute across providers including AWS, Hetzner, and Linode.
